<!-- Generated by scripts/sync_chebfun_examples.py. -->
<!-- Source: https://www.chebfun.org/examples/roots/BesselRoots.html -->

<div class="chebfun-import chebfun-example-import">
<div class='page-header'>
<h1>Roots of a Bessel function</h1>
<h2>Nick Trefethen, September 2010 in <a href='../'>roots</a><span><a href='/examples/roots/BesselRoots.m'>download</a><span class='sep-sm'>&middot;</span><a href='//github.com/chebfun/examples/blob/master/roots/BesselRoots.m'>view on GitHub</a></span></h2>
</div>

<div id='content' class="col-sm-12 example" role="main">
<p>[revised June 2019]</p>
<p>Here is the Bessel function $J_0$ on the interval $[0,100]$.</p>
<pre class="mcode-input">J0 = chebfun(@(x) besselj(0,x),[0 100]);
figure, plot(J0), grid on
title('Bessel function J_0')</pre>

<p><img src="../../../images/roots/BesselRoots_01.png" class="figure chebfun-figure" alt=""></p>
<p>We can find its roots like this:</p>
<pre class="mcode-input">r = roots(J0);
hold on, plot(r,J0(r),'.r')</pre>

<p><img src="../../../images/roots/BesselRoots_02.png" class="figure chebfun-figure" alt=""></p>
<p>The number of roots can be found with the <code>length</code> command:</p>
<pre class="mcode-input">number_of_roots = length(r)</pre>

<pre class="mcode-output">number_of_roots =
    32
</pre>

<p>Suppose you wanted to know the numbers of roots in various intervals $[a,b]$. You could define an anonymous function:</p>
<pre class="mcode-input">rootsab = @(a,b) length(roots(chebfun(@(x) besselj(0,x),[a b])));</pre>

<p>For example:</p>
<pre class="mcode-input">tic
disp('Number of roots between 1000000 and 1001000:')
n = rootsab(1000000,1001000)
toc</pre>

<pre class="mcode-output">Number of roots between 1000000 and 1001000:
n =
   318
Elapsed time is 0.077299 seconds.
</pre></div>
        </div>
    </div>
</div>
